sglang.title: [router] rust-based router
sglang.author: ByronHsu
sglang.bodyExcerpt:
Motivation
#1732
This pull request follows up on #1695 and introduces a new router implementation in Rust that achieves a 2x speedup compared to the old Python router. It achieves comparable performance to our current Data Parallel (--dp) implementation (can be further enhanced by moving the interme

sglang.title: Fix update_weights deadlock for DP
sglang.author: ByronHsu
sglang.bodyExcerpt:
Motivation

Fix #1814
In the current DP, when we call update weights

tokenizer sends a req to dp controller
dp controller sends reqs to "all" schedulers
scheduler updates weights and send response to detokenizer
"all" dokenizer sends response to tokenizer

However, the current code assumes there is

sglang.title: Support setting `use_thread` in the `run_program` for easier debugging.
sglang.author: liuyanyi
sglang.bodyExcerpt:
Motivation

When attempting to debug using VSCode within the sglang function, all breakpoints cause the VSCode debugger to become unresponsive after the first exec of llm. Through step-by-step testing, it appears that the use of threads in StreamExecutor is causing the issue.
For example, this code

vllm.title: [Bugfix] Fix ray instance detect issue
vllm.author: yma11
vllm.bodyExcerpt: Fix ray instance detect so that will first try connecting to latest launched instance and if not, create a new one with num_gpus=parallel_config.world_size.

vllm.title: [CI/Build] Adopt Mergify for auto-labeling PRs
vllm.author: russellb
vllm.bodyExcerpt:
Import initial configuration for Mergify.
This configuration does the following:


Auto-label PRs that change docs (docs/ directory or any file
ending in .md) with the documentation label


Auto-label PRs that change CI/Build related files with the ci/build label.


Auto-label PRs with the frontend

vllm.title: [Doc] fix third-party model example
vllm.author: russellb
vllm.bodyExcerpt:
The docs give some sample code for running with a third-party model.
This example doesn't work with the spawn multiprocessing method. In
that case, you get a recursive run of the runpy.run_module() call.
Wrapping that call in a __main__ guard avoids this problem.
Closes #9763
Signed-off-by: Russell
